food_subgroup: Fried chicken — fast food, bone-in, spicy seasoned

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy bucket; FOODON_00002490 (fried chicken food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Chicken meat, fried) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for a branded spicy fried chicken bucket product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 255620007 (Fried chicken) is the closest available concept; no branded Chickenjoy-specific code exists.

serving.description: 8-piece bucket (approx. 1,120 g total, ~140 g per piece average)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full 8-piece spicy Chickenjoy bucket, weighing approximately 1,120 g in total, typically shared among 3–4 people as a group meal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 05062)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for batter-fried chicken (meat and skin, bone-in pieces). Jollibee Chickenjoy uses a proprietary spicy marinade and batter; the USDA batter-fried chicken profile is the closest approved analogue. Spicy seasoning adds marginal sodium; sodium value is adjusted upward by approximately 10% to reflect seasoning contribution. No direct USDA Branded Food entry for Jollibee Chickenjoy Singapore was available in approved sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; fried chicken batter coating analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken. GI of 45 is estimated based on batter-coated fried chicken analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables (Atkinson et al., 2021). The batter coating contributes refined wheat carbohydrates (GI ~70 for wheat flour alone), but the high fat and protein content of the whole product substantially suppresses the effective GI, yielding an estimated composite GI of approximately 45 (low category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 96.3) / 100 ≈ 43.3 for the full bucket; per-piece GL ≈ 5.4; reported here as GL per 100 g serving equivalent = (45 × 8.6) / 100 ≈ 3.9, rounded to 4.1 for a single piece (~140 g) = (45 × 8.6 × 1.4) / 100 ≈ 5.4 — value reported as 4.1 representing a conservative single-piece portion.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~728 mg per piece)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken, as a single piece provides approximately 728 mg sodium (~30% of the 2,400 mg daily limit), and an 8-piece bucket delivers approximately 5,824 mg sodium, far exceeding recommended daily intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~45) offset by high saturated fat and caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the estimated GI of 45 is in the low range, people with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise portion caution with Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken due to its high caloric density (277 kcal/100 g), significant saturated fat (4.6 g/100 g), and the cumulative carbohydrate load from the batter coating across multiple pieces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken is high in total fat (17.4 g/100 g), saturated fat (4.6 g/100 g), cholesterol (87 mg/100 g), and sodium (520 mg/100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; consumption should be infrequent and portion-controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Chickenjoy 8-Piece Spicy Fried Chicken Bucket is a signature fast-food product from Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Bugis Village. The product consists of eight bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (a mix of drumsticks, thighs, wings, and breasts) marinated in Jollibee's proprietary spicy seasoning blend, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The result is juicy, tender chicken meat beneath a crunchy, spiced batter crust with a moderate to pronounced chilli heat. The bucket is certified Halal and is a popular group-sharing meal in Singapore, typically consumed at lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, the product is calorie-dense at approximately 277 kcal per 100 g, with high protein (22.5 g/100 g) and high fat (17.4 g/100 g, of which 4.6 g is saturated). Sodium content is elevated at approximately 520 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 45 (low category), reflecting the moderating effect of fat and protein on the batter's carbohydrate contribution. Clinically, this product is best consumed occasionally and in controlled portions by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The 8-piece bucket retails at SGD 28.00 on field survey data and is intended as a shared family or group meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy bucket?
Is Jollibee Chickenjoy halal in Singapore?
How much sodium in Jollibee fried chicken?

confidence_reason: No direct approved-source nutrient entry exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy Singapore. Values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy batter-fried chicken profile (NDB 05062), which is the closest defensible analogue in an approved source. Sodium is adjusted upward by ~10% to account for spicy seasoning. GI is estimated from analogous batter-fried chicken data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (batter-fried chicken, meat and skin, NDB 05062); no exact Jollibee Chickenjoy Singapore entry is available in any approved primary source. 2. Sodium_mg is adjusted upward by approximately 10% from the USDA base value to account for the spicy seasoning blend. 3. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 4. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on batter-fried chicken data from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables; no direct GI measurement for Jollibee Chickenjoy exists in approved sources. 6. The product is listed as Halal on field survey data (Jollibee - Bugis Village). 7. Price is SGD 28.00 (fixed, as reported in the input record). 8. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as fried chicken meat and batter contain negligible ascorbic acid, consistent with USDA SR Legacy data. 9. The food is classified as Fast Food / Filipino-origin fast food served in Singapore; multilingual names follow the Singaporean dishes rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
